decision_summary: The chip temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the 85.0°C limit. Since geometry changes are hitting legality limits and recent improvements have stalled, the strategy shifts to material tuning. Increasing the heat spreader conductivity is the highest-priority lever to enhance lateral heat spreading and reduce peak temperature.

changes.reason: Doubling the spreader conductivity (within the allowed 2.0x ratio) will significantly improve lateral heat distribution away from the chip center, lowering the peak temperature.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip_max_temperature towards or below 85.0°C
More uniform temperature profile across the heat spreader
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if the bottleneck shifts to the base plate conductivity or boundary conditions
High conductivity values may require finer meshing in future iterations to resolve gradients accurately
